what: 2026-03-02 — Normalize api_key handling; add live contract tests
title: Development Notes — openalexPro
text: Background: API key handling diverged across functions docs. code paths assumed key always required, OpenAlex still supports unauthenticated requests lower limits. consistent contract needed: allow explicit unauthenticated mode validating malformed inputs. Implementation: pro_count() pro_request() / fetch_query_pages() pro_fetch() pro_download_content() pro_rate_limit_status() pro_validate_credentials() docs api_key = NULL api_key = "" => send request without API key query param. Otherwise api_key must length-1 character string. Invalid types now fail fast clear error. Updated roxygen/man docs functions reflect optional unauthenticated mode. Testing: tests/testthat/helper_live.R tests/testthat/test-900-live_api_contracts.R OPENALEXPRO_LIVE_TESTS=true openalexPro.apikey set non-dummy value Patched test-014-pro_download_content.R reflect new behavior api_key missing (longer immediate error; request sent without key). Full suite passes normalization. Cassette workflow hardening: Added inst/scripts/record_cassettes.R re-record sanitize cassettes. Added preflight key check via pro_rate_limit_status() avoid replacing valid cassettes HTTP 401 responses credentials invalid. Updated helper_vcr.R behavior explicit recording mode (OPENALEXPRO_RECORD_CASSETTES).

title: Development Notes — openalexPro
text: Background: OpenAlex added dedicated /rate-limit endpoint returns caller’s current daily budget, amount used, remaining balance, per-endpoint costs single JSON object. replaces previous credential-check pattern (making dummy pro_count() call) purpose-built status function. Implementation: New file R/pro_rate_limit_status.R exported pro_rate_limit_status(). Makes GET https://api.openalex.org/rate-limit?api_key=<key> request. 200 → parse body, return list invisibly; print summary verbose = TRUE. 401/403 → message “Invalid API key …”, return FALSE invisibly. Network/connection error (caught tryCatch) → message “Request failed …”, return NULL invisibly. Missing key (empty string) → message setup instructions, return FALSE. (Updated 2026-03-02) Now goes api_call(get_html_response = NULL) wrapped suppressMessages() — see refactor entry rationale. @importFrom httr2 updated drop req_error (now handled inside api_call). Refactor: pro_validate_credentials() now calls pro_rate_limit_status(api_key = api_key, verbose = FALSE) checks isTRUE(.list(result)). public interface (messages, TRUE/FALSE return) unchanged. Using rate-limit endpoint semantically correct avoids wasting quota dummy count query. Tests: tests/testthat/test-015-pro_rate_limit_status.R two VCR cassettes (pro_rate_limit_status_200.yml, pro_rate_limit_status_401.yml). Covers: missing key, invalid key (401), success verbose=TRUE/FALSE, network error via local_mocked_bindings. 13 assertions, green. Key files: R/pro_rate_limit_status.R, R/pro_validate_credentials.R, tests/testthat/test-015-pro_rate_limit_status.R, tests/fixtures/vcr/pro_rate_limit_status_200.yml, tests/fixtures/vcr/pro_rate_limit_status_401.yml

what: 2026-03-02 — Unify API calls through api_call(); document XPAC
title: Development Notes — openalexPro
text: Background: Two functions (pro_rate_limit_status pro_download_content) building httr2 pipelines inline req_retry() req_error() calls, duplicating logic already centralised api_call(). separate set documentation gaps identified around OpenAlex XPAC support. API call unification: functions now call api_call(req, ..., get_html_response = NULL). get_html_response = NULL tells api_call return (abort) non-200 responses; caller inspects status code . suppressMessages() wraps api_call() call helper’s internal logging ("⚠ HTTP 401 - returning response caller inspection", etc.) suppressed; function emits user-facing messages instead. pro_download_content() gains User-Agent header (previously missing). pro_download_content() passes max_retries = 5 (matching previous req_retry(max_tries = 5)); transient_responses defaults identical. pro_rate_limit_status()’s tryCatch catches rlang::abort thrown api_call() network errors, returning NULL . 32 tests (014 + 015) green refactor. Key insight — get_html_response = NULL vs missing: api_call() uses R’s missing() detect whether argument provided. Passing NULL explicitly selects “return non-200 responses”; omitting argument entirely selects “abort non-200”. correct mode callers. XPAC documentation: Added ### XPAC — Expansion Pack Works subsection vignettes/pro_query.qmd ## Additional Options. Explains XPAC , enable via options = list(include_xpac = TRUE), filter XPAC-works is_xpac = TRUE, chain discovery pro_download_content(). code changes needed — options parameter already splices arbitrary query parameters via httr2::req_url_query(req, !!!q). Added XPAC example pro_download_content() @examples roxygen block. Key files: R/pro_rate_limit_status.R, R/pro_download_content.R, vignettes/pro_query.qmd

what: 2026-02-25 — Add pro_download_content() for PDF and TEI XML downloads
title: Development Notes — openalexPro
text: Background: OpenAlex added new content endpoint (content.openalex.org) serves full-text PDFs (~60 M files) Grobid TEI XML (~43 M files) works. Cost $0.01 per file. metadata API already supports has_content.pdf:true has_content.grobid-xml:true filter arguments pro_query() discover downloadable works. Implementation: - New file R/pro_download_content.R exported pro_download_content(). - Accepts character vector work IDs (full OpenAlex URLs, normalised). - Downloads via httr2::req_perform(): PDF → resp_body_raw() + writeBin(); grobid-xml → resp_body_string() + writeLines(). - Parallel via future_lapply() consistent rest package. - Returns data frame (id, file, status, message) — never aborts partial failures. - API key passed ?api_key= query parameter (functions). - Tests use httr2::with_mocked_responses() — VCR cassettes needed. Key file: R/pro_download_content.R

title: Development Notes — openalexPro
text: Root cause: Windows GitHub Actions runners, tempdir() returns path containing 8.3 short name (RUNNER~1), normalizePath(f) file inside directory resolves long name (runneradmin). structurally different strings even though refer location. code compared absolute paths (via %%, !=, regex substitution) resume detection subdir logic silently malfunction. Additionally, normalizePath() uses \ separator Windows, list.files() DuckDB use /, causing %% comparisons fail even short/long-name issue absent. Symptoms (12 test failures Windows CI, none macOS/Linux): test-013 (resume detection snapshot_to_parquet()): %% comparing list.files() output (/) vs normalizePath() output (\) always FALSE → files re-converted instead skipped. test-012 (build_corpus_index() + lookup_by_id() path doubling): build_corpus_index() used regexp_replace(filename, '<snapshot_dir>', '') SQL. Windows, snapshot_dir contained \, DuckDB filename uses / → regex never matched → full absolute path stored. lookup_by_id() file.path(snapshot_path, abs_path) doubling path. test-004/test-007 (spurious query= directories pro_request_jsonl_parquet()): dirname(normalizePath(f)) != input_root always TRUE due 8.3 vs long-name mismatch → every output file placed query=<dirname> subdirectory instead root output directory. Fixes: R/snapshot_to_parquet.R: Apply gsub("\\\\", "/", ...) sides %% comparison used resume detection (existing parquets vs expected parquets). R/build_corpus_index.R: Compute snapshot_depth (number path components snapshot_dir) future_lapply. Inside worker, split file path components extract pf_parts[seq(snapshot_depth + 1, length(pf_parts))] relative path. Pass SQL literal string ('<rel_path>' parquet_file) — filename = true needed, regex SQL. R/pro_request_jsonl_parquet.R: Replace normalizePath string comparison depth-based subdir detection: compute input_depth, split file path, check length(f_parts) > input_depth + 1 detect subdirectories. R/lookup_by_id.R: Normalize separators file.path(): gsub("\\\\", "/", file.path(snapshot_path, names(file_chunks))). Key principle: Path-depth counting immune 8.3 vs long-name differences RUNNER~1 runneradmin occupy depth hierarchy. Never compare absolute paths across normalizePath() list.files() Windows. 183 tests pass macOS fixes; Windows CI longer path-related failures. Key files: R/snapshot_to_parquet.R, R/build_corpus_index.R, R/pro_request_jsonl_parquet.R, R/lookup_by_id.R

what: 2026-02-23 — Preserve abstract_inverted_index as VARCHAR in works parquet
title: Development Notes — openalexPro
text: Problem: OpenAlex works files contain abstract_inverted_index JSON keys "" "" (case-different, valid JSON). DuckDB folds struct field names lowercase, causing collision (duplicate key "") crashes conversion. ignore_errors=true silently drops entire records rather just problematic field, destructive. Dropping column entirely loses data. Root cause: DuckDB auto-infers JSON objects STRUCT types case-folds field names. Arrow R JSON reading capability, used alternative. Fix: schema inference, override type abstract_inverted_index VARCHAR columns_clause works dataset. DuckDB reads field type VARCHAR, captures raw JSON text without parsing object’s keys struct fields — case-folding, collision. records preserved. stored VARCHAR value JSON string (e.g. '{"":[0],"":[1],"":[2]}'), parseable jsonlite::fromJSON() needed. Case-sensitive keys preserved ’re stored data, struct field names. Changes: - R/snapshot_to_parquet.R: replaces column-drop gsub type-override gsub ('abstract_inverted_index': 'VARCHAR'); ignore_errors=true removed ndjson_options (longer needed). - tests/testthat/test-013-snapshot_to_parquet.R: added test verifying column present character ""/"" round-trip correctly via jsonlite. Key file: R/snapshot_to_parquet.R

what: 2026-02-23 — Per-file schema inference with two-level disk caching
title: Development Notes — openalexPro
text: Problem: infer_json_schema() ran single bulk DuckDB query sampled files: DESCRIBE SELECT * read_json_auto([N files], union_by_name = true, ...). works dataset (1981 files, maximum_object_size=1000000000), OOMed killed R (exit code -1) sample_size exceeded file count sampling occurred. Fix: Replaced single bulk query per-file loop — one small DESCRIBE per file — followed R-side schema merging. Added two levels disk caching: Per-file schema cache (<parquet_ds>/.schema_cache/<update_date>_<part_name>.csv): file’s schema saved immediately inference, mid-inference restarts skip already-processed files. Unified schema cache (<parquet_ds>/.schema_cache/unified_schema.csv): merged result saved per-file schemas merged; loaded first subsequent run, bypassing DuckDB queries entirely. Delete file force re-inference. Type-widening rules merge_schemas() (columns differ across files): 1. identical → keep -. 2. STRUCT/LIST/MAP vs simpler type → complex type wins. 3. Multiple STRUCT types → pick one fields. 4. Numeric conflicts → widest type wins: TINYINT < SMALLINT < INTEGER < BIGINT < HUGEINT < FLOAT < DOUBLE. 5. Fallback → VARCHAR. Column order preserved (first-seen, alphabetical) maintain backward compatibility existing parquet consumers. Changes: - R/infer_json_schema.R: rewrote infer_json_schema() use per-file loop caching; added schema_cache_dir parameter; added merge_schemas() internal helper. - R/snapshot_to_parquet.R: passes schema_cache_dir = file.path(parquet_ds, ".schema_cache"). - tests/testthat/test-013-snapshot_to_parquet.R: added two new tests cache creation unified schema reuse. Key files: R/infer_json_schema.R, R/snapshot_to_parquet.R, tests/testthat/test-013-snapshot_to_parquet.R

what: 2026-02-13 — snapshot_to_parquet path fixes and Makefile improvements
title: Development Notes — openalexPro
text: Motivation (path collision bug): original code used basename() derive output parquet filenames input .gz filenames. OpenAlex snapshots use hive partitioning (e.g. updated_date=2024-01-01/part_000.gz), multiple partitions contain identically-named files. Using basename() caused output collisions. Fix: Replaced basename() relative path computation: nchar(root) + 1 = path separator position; +2 = first character relative path. Avoids regex escaping entirely. Motivation (hive partition naming pro_request_jsonl_parquet): Query result subfolders (e.g. Chunk_1, may contain spaces) become proper hive partition directories (query=Chunk_1) parquet output compatibility Arrow/DuckDB partition-aware reads. Fix pro_request_jsonl_parquet.R: Makefile improvements (inst/Makefile.snapshot): - Added SAMPLE_SIZE=10000 variable (passed snapshot_to_parquet()). - Added cli.progress_handlers_force = "cli" Rscript options progress bars render non-interactive (Makefile) sessions. - Added SAMPLE_SIZE help output override example line. Key files: R/snapshot_to_parquet.R, R/pro_request_jsonl_parquet.R, inst/Makefile.snapshot, tests/testthat/test-013-snapshot_to_parquet.R
code: json_dir_norm <- normalizePath(json_dir) rel_paths <- vapply(gz_files, function(f) {   substring(normalizePath(f), nchar(json_dir_norm) + 2) }, character(1), USE.NAMES = FALSE) if (dirname(f_norm) != input_root) {   file.path(output, paste0("query=", basename(dirname(f_norm))), fname) } else {   file.path(output, fname) }

title: Development Notes — openalexPro
text: Motivation: original snapshot_to_parquet() loaded .gz files dataset single DuckDB query, caused OOM errors large datasets. refactor converts .gz one .parquet file individually, enabling: Parallelisation via future_lapply() one DuckDB connection per worker. Per-file resume: already-converted files skipped re-run. Unified schema: sample files used infer consistent schema conversion, preventing type conflicts across parquet files. Architecture: infer_json_schema() convert_json_to_parquet() live R/infer_json_schema.R (internal, @noRd). Schema inference detail: Uses DuckDB’s DESCRIBE SELECT * read_json_auto([...], union_by_name = true) sample files. resulting columns clause ({'col': 'TYPE', ...}) passed read_json(..., columns = ...) file, ensuring output parquets share schema even individual files missing columns. Special case: works dataset uses maximum_object_size=1000000000 individual work records can exceed DuckDB’s default JSON object size limit. Key files: R/snapshot_to_parquet.R, R/infer_json_schema.R
code: snapshot_to_parquet()         # main loop over datasets   → infer_json_schema()       # Stage 1: DESCRIBE on sample of files   → future_lapply(            # Stage 2: parallel conversion       convert_json_to_parquet() # one DuckDB connection per file     )

text: Motivation: OpenAlex snapshot conversion ~300 GB parquet files. Loading memory look specific IDs feasible. dedicated index (_id_idx.parquet) stores (id, source_file, row_group) tuples, enabling fast seeks. Architecture: Key files: R/build_corpus_index.R, R/lookup_by_id.R

text: openalexPro package enables downloading processing large scholarly datasets OpenAlex. Unlike openalexR processes everything memory, openalexPro writes results disk, enabling analysis datasets large fit RAM. Processing uses DuckDB efficient queries without loading entire datasets memory.

from inverted index] T2[Generate citation string
Smith et al. 2023] T3[Add page field
for provenance] end subgraph Output[JSONL Output] Abstract[abstract: full text] Citation[citation: string] Page[page: identifier] OtherOut[other fields...] end IAI --> T1 --> Abstract Auth --> T2 --> Citation Other --> T3 --> Page Other --> OtherOut style Transform fill:#fff3cd # Convert JSONL to Parquet with schema harmonization pro_request_jsonl_parquet( input_jsonl = "data/jsonl", output = "data/parquet", progress = TRUE, sample_size = 1000, # Records to sample for schema inference delete_input = FALSE # Keep JSONL files ) flowchart TD subgraph Input[JSONL Files with Varying Schemas] F1["File 1: location = struct"] F2["File 2: location = string"] F3["File 3: location = null"] end subgraph Process[Schema Harmonization] Sample[Sample records
from all files] Infer[Infer unified schema
using DuckDB] Apply[Apply schema
to all files] end subgraph Output[Parquet Dataset] Unified["Unified schema
location = struct"] end F1 --> Sample F2 --> Sample F3 --> Sample Sample --> Infer Infer --> Apply Apply --> Unified style Process fill:#cce5ff style Output fill:#e1f5e1 library(duckdb) library(DBI) con <- dbConnect(duckdb()) # Top cited papers top_cited <- dbGetQuery( con, " SELECT title, publication_year, cited_by_count FROM read_parquet('data/parquet/**/*.parquet') WHERE cited_by_count IS NOT NULL ORDER BY cited_by_count DESC LIMIT 20 " ) # Papers per year by_year <- dbGetQuery( con, " SELECT publication_year, COUNT(*) as count FROM read_parquet('data/parquet/**/*.parquet') GROUP BY publication_year ORDER BY publication_year " ) # Search abstracts keyword_search <- dbGetQuery( con, " SELECT title, abstract, cited_by_count FROM read_parquet('data/parquet/**/*.parquet') WHERE abstract LIKE '%machine learning%' ORDER BY cited_by_count DESC LIMIT 10 " ) dbDisconnect(con)

title: Workflow Guide
text: Analyze research output multiple institutions: Build citation network set papers: Track research trends time: Analyze concepts appear together: OpenAlex provides full-text PDFs (~60 M works) Grobid TEI XML (~43 M works) via content.openalex.org. Use pro_download_content() retrieve . file costs $0.01. pro_download_content() never aborts partial failures — inspect status column identify files need retrying. TEI XML (structured full text parsed Grobid):
code: # MIT, Stanford, Harvard institutions <- c("I63966007", "I97018004", "I136199984")  # Build queries for each urls <- lapply(institutions, function(inst) {   pro_query(     entity = "works",     `institutions.id` = inst,     from_publication_date = "2020-01-01",     type = "article",     select = c("ids", "title", "publication_year", "cited_by_count", "concepts")   ) }) names(urls) <- c("MIT", "Stanford", "Harvard")  # Download each for (name in names(urls)) {   pro_fetch(     query_url = urls[[name]],     project_folder = file.path("institutions", name),     progress = TRUE   ) }  # Compare with DuckDB library(duckdb) con <- dbConnect(duckdb())  comparison <- dbGetQuery(   con,   "   SELECT     'MIT' as institution,     COUNT(*) as papers,     AVG(cited_by_count) as avg_citations   FROM read_parquet('institutions/MIT/parquet/**/*.parquet')   UNION ALL   SELECT     'Stanford',     COUNT(*),     AVG(cited_by_count)   FROM read_parquet('institutions/Stanford/parquet/**/*.parquet')   UNION ALL   SELECT     'Harvard',     COUNT(*),     AVG(cited_by_count)   FROM read_parquet('institutions/Harvard/parquet/**/*.parquet') " )  dbDisconnect(con) # Start with seed papers seed_dois <- c(   "10.1038/nature12373",   "10.1126/science.1259855" )  # Get seed papers seed_url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   doi = seed_dois,   select = c("ids", "title", "cited_by_count", "referenced_works") )  pro_fetch(   query_url = seed_url,   project_folder = "citation_network/seeds" )  # Get papers that cite the seeds for (doi in seed_dois) {   citing_url <- pro_query(     entity = "works",     cites = paste0("https://doi.org/", doi),     select = c("ids", "title", "publication_year", "cited_by_count")   )    pro_fetch(     query_url = citing_url,     project_folder = paste0("citation_network/citing_", gsub("/", "_", doi))   ) } # Get publication counts by year using group_by url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "artificial intelligence",   from_publication_date = "2000-01-01",   group_by = "publication_year" )  # For group_by queries, use pro_request directly pro_request(   query_url = url,   output = "ai_trends/json",   progress = TRUE )  # The JSON contains aggregated counts, not individual works url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "climate change",   from_publication_date = "2023-01-01",   type = "article",   select = c("ids", "title", "concepts") )  pro_fetch(   query_url = url,   project_folder = "concept_analysis" )  # Analyze concept co-occurrence with DuckDB library(duckdb) con <- dbConnect(duckdb())  # Unnest concepts and count concept_counts <- dbGetQuery(   con,   "   WITH unnested AS (     SELECT       id,       unnest(concepts) as concept     FROM read_parquet('concept_analysis/parquet/**/*.parquet')   )   SELECT     concept.display_name as concept_name,     concept.level as concept_level,     COUNT(*) as count   FROM unnested   GROUP BY concept.display_name, concept.level   ORDER BY count DESC   LIMIT 50 " )  dbDisconnect(con) # Step 1: find works that have a PDF available url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   `has_content.pdf` = TRUE,   `best_oa_location.license` = c("cc-by", "cc-by-sa"),   from_publication_date = "2023-01-01",   type = "article",   select = c("ids", "title", "publication_year") )  # Step 2: download metadata to get the work IDs pro_fetch(   query_url = url,   project_folder = "oa_works" )  # Step 3: read IDs from the parquet dataset library(duckdb) con <- dbConnect(duckdb()) ids <- dbGetQuery(   con,   "SELECT id FROM read_parquet('oa_works/parquet/**/*.parquet')" )$id dbDisconnect(con)  # Step 4: download full-text PDFs results <- pro_download_content(   ids     = ids,   output  = "oa_works/pdfs",   format  = "pdf",   workers = 4         # parallel downloads )  # results is a data frame: id | file | status | message table(results$status) # ok        not_found  error # 892       45         3 results_xml <- pro_download_content(   ids    = ids,   output = "oa_works/xml",   format = "grobid-xml" )

title: pro_query() - Background and Technical Documentation
text: basic query requires entity type: pro_query() supports three mutually exclusive search parameters, different matching behaviour cost: Searches title, abstract, full text stemming stop-word removal. Supports boolean/phrase syntax search.exact also applies stemming (e.g. “run” also matches “running”, “ran”): Searches without stemming stop-word removal. Supports: Boolean operators: , , Quoted phrases: "deep learning" Proximity: "climate change"~5 (within 5 words) Wildcards: microbio* Converts query 1 024-dimension embedding returns conceptually similar works — regardless whether share keywords. OpenAlex’s “find similar works” feature. Constraints: Maximum 50 results per call (API hard-codes ). Requires API key (costs $0.001 per call). Rate-limited 1 request per second. Pass sentence short paragraph — just keyword. Note: Semantic search returns 50 results. large-scale retrieval, combine search search.exact pro_fetch(). Use select parameter specify fields return. reduces response size improves performance: Use opt_select_fields() see available fields: Fetch specific entity OpenAlex ID: can use identifier listed entity’s ids field — just OpenAlex ID. API accepts three formats: Works — DOI: Authors — ORCID: Institutions — ROR: Sources (journals) — ISSN: Tip: bulk lookup multiple DOIs IDs, use filter (doi = c(...)) rather id parameter — pro_query() automatically chunk batches 50.
code: library(openalexPro)  # Query for works url <- pro_query(entity = "works") url # [1] "https://api.openalex.org/works" url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "climate change biodiversity" ) url # [1] "https://api.openalex.org/works?search=climate%20change%20biodiversity" # Exact phrase search url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search.exact = '"large language models" AND (safety OR alignment)' )  # Wildcard — matches microbiology, microbiome, microbiota, … url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search.exact = "microbio*",   from_publication_date = "2020-01-01" ) # Find works conceptually similar to an abstract or research question url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search.semantic = paste(     "Large language models trained on scientific text can assist researchers",     "in hypothesis generation and literature synthesis."   ) )  # Combine with filters to narrow the similarity search url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search.semantic = "CRISPR base editing for treating sickle cell disease",   from_publication_date = "2019-01-01",   type = "article",   select = c("ids", "title", "publication_year", "cited_by_count") ) url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "machine learning",   select = c("ids", "title", "publication_year", "cited_by_count") ) url # [1] "https://api.openalex.org/works?search=machine%20learning&select=ids,title,publication_year,cited_by_count" opt_select_fields() # [1] "abstract_inverted_index" "authorships" # [3] "biblio"                  "cited_by_count" # [5] "concepts"                "corresponding_author_ids" # ... url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   id = "W2741809807" ) url # [1] "https://api.openalex.org/works/W2741809807" # By DOI (full URL format) url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   id = "https://doi.org/10.7717/peerj.4375" )  # By DOI (namespace format) url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   id = "doi:10.7717/peerj.4375" ) url <- pro_query(   entity = "authors",   id = "orcid:0000-0003-1613-5981" ) url <- pro_query(   entity = "institutions",   id = "ror:02mhbdp94"  # MIT ) url <- pro_query(   entity = "sources",   id = "issn:0028-0836"  # Nature )

title: pro_query() - Background and Technical Documentation
text: Converts query 1 024-dimension embedding returns conceptually similar works — regardless whether share keywords. OpenAlex’s “find similar works” feature. code:
flowchart TD subgraph FilterSyntax[Filter Syntax in URL] Single["filter=type:article"] Multiple["filter=type:article,language:en"] OR["filter=type:article|preprint"] Combined["filter=type:article|preprint,language:en|de"] end subgraph RCode[R Code Equivalent] RSingle["type = 'article'"] RMultiple["type = 'article',
language = 'en'"] ROR["type = c('article', 'preprint')"] RCombined["type = c('article', 'preprint'),
language = c('en', 'de')"] end RSingle --> Single RMultiple --> Multiple ROR --> OR RCombined --> Combined style FilterSyntax fill:#cce5ff style RCode fill:#e1f5e1 url <- pro_query( entity = "works", from_publication_date = "2020-01-01", to_publication_date = "2023-12-31", type = "article" ) url # filter=from_publication_date:2020-01-01,to_publication_date:2023-12-31,type:article url <- pro_query( entity = "works", language = c("en", "de", "fr"), # English OR German OR French type = c("article", "preprint") # Article OR Preprint ) url # filter=language:en|de|fr,type:article|preprint # Works from 2020 onwards pro_query(entity = "works", from_publication_date = "2020-01-01") # Works in a specific year pro_query(entity = "works", publication_year = 2023) # Works in a date range pro_query( entity = "works", from_publication_date = "2020-01-01", to_publication_date = "2020-12-31" ) # Highly cited works (100+ citations) pro_query(entity = "works", from_cited_by_count = 100) # Citation range pro_query( entity = "works", from_cited_by_count = 10, to_cited_by_count = 100 ) # Only open access works pro_query(entity = "works", is_oa = TRUE) # Specific OA status pro_query(entity = "works", oa_status = "gold") pro_query(entity = "works", oa_status = c("gold", "green")) # Works by a specific author (use backticks for dots) pro_query(entity = "works", `author.id` = "A2208157607") # Works from a specific institution pro_query(entity = "works", `institutions.id` = "I4200000001") # Works with a specific affiliation country pro_query(entity = "works", `institutions.country_code` = "US") # Works about machine learning pro_query(entity = "works", `concepts.id` = "C119857082") # Works in multiple fields pro_query(entity = "works", `concepts.id` = c("C119857082", "C41008148"))

title: pro_query() - Background and Technical Documentation
text: Use group_by get aggregate counts instead individual records: group_by query returns list groups rather individual records. group three fields: API returns 200 groups per page. default, entities value grouped field hidden. Append :include_unknown expose separate group key = "unknown": options parameter accepts additional query parameters: OpenAlex XPAC (“expansion pack”) adds approximately 190 million additional works DataCite institutional/subject repositories standard ~278 million works, bringing total ~470 million. works excluded API responses default avoid disrupting existing queries; tend lower metadata quality standard corpus, though quality improving time. Enable XPAC passing include_xpac = TRUE via options parameter: retrieve XPAC works (excluding standard works), combine include_xpac = TRUE is_xpac filter: XPAC works can combined filter search mode, including semantic search: XPAC work IDs can passed directly pro_download_content() — include_xpac discovery-phase parameter needed download time. find XPAC works also downloadable full-text, combine filters: See OpenAlex XPAC documentation details.
code: url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "artificial intelligence",   group_by = "publication_year" ) # Returns counts per year instead of individual works # Count works by OA status, including works with unknown status url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "climate change",   group_by = "oa_status:include_unknown" ) url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "quantum computing",   options = list(     per_page = 200, # Results per page (max 200)     sort = "cited_by_count:desc", # Sort by citations descending     cursor = "*", # Enable cursor pagination     sample = 100 # Random sample of 100 works   ) ) # Sort by publication date (newest first) pro_query(entity = "works", options = list(sort = "publication_date:desc"))  # Sort by citation count (highest first) pro_query(entity = "works", options = list(sort = "cited_by_count:desc"))  # Sort by relevance (default for search queries) pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "climate",   options = list(sort = "relevance_score:desc") ) # Include XPAC works in results (~470 M total instead of ~278 M) url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "grey literature",   options = list(include_xpac = TRUE) ) # Query XPAC-only works (must also set include_xpac = TRUE) url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   is_xpac = TRUE,   from_publication_date = "2024-01-01",   options = list(include_xpac = TRUE) ) # Semantic search across the full XPAC-inclusive corpus url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search.semantic = "institutional repository preprint data management",   options = list(include_xpac = TRUE) ) # Find XPAC works with PDFs, then download them urls <- pro_query(   entity          = "works",   has_content.pdf = TRUE,   is_xpac         = TRUE,   from_publication_date = "2024-01-01",   options = list(include_xpac = TRUE) ) # After fetching and extracting IDs from the metadata: # result <- pro_download_content(ids = xpac_ids, format = "pdf")

code: url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "artificial intelligence",   group_by = "publication_year" ) # Returns counts per year instead of individual works # Count works by OA status, including works with unknown status url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "climate change",   group_by = "oa_status:include_unknown" )

text: options parameter accepts additional query parameters: OpenAlex XPAC (“expansion pack”) adds approximately 190 million additional works DataCite institutional/subject repositories standard ~278 million works, bringing total ~470 million. works excluded API responses default avoid disrupting existing queries; tend lower metadata quality standard corpus, though quality improving time. Enable XPAC passing include_xpac = TRUE via options parameter: retrieve XPAC works (excluding standard works), combine include_xpac = TRUE is_xpac filter: XPAC works can combined filter search mode, including semantic search: XPAC work IDs can passed directly pro_download_content() — include_xpac discovery-phase parameter needed download time. find XPAC works also downloadable full-text, combine filters: See OpenAlex XPAC documentation details.
code: url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "quantum computing",   options = list(     per_page = 200, # Results per page (max 200)     sort = "cited_by_count:desc", # Sort by citations descending     cursor = "*", # Enable cursor pagination     sample = 100 # Random sample of 100 works   ) ) # Sort by publication date (newest first) pro_query(entity = "works", options = list(sort = "publication_date:desc"))  # Sort by citation count (highest first) pro_query(entity = "works", options = list(sort = "cited_by_count:desc"))  # Sort by relevance (default for search queries) pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "climate",   options = list(sort = "relevance_score:desc") ) # Include XPAC works in results (~470 M total instead of ~278 M) url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search = "grey literature",   options = list(include_xpac = TRUE) ) # Query XPAC-only works (must also set include_xpac = TRUE) url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   is_xpac = TRUE,   from_publication_date = "2024-01-01",   options = list(include_xpac = TRUE) ) # Semantic search across the full XPAC-inclusive corpus url <- pro_query(   entity = "works",   search.semantic = "institutional repository preprint data management",   options = list(include_xpac = TRUE) ) # Find XPAC works with PDFs, then download them urls <- pro_query(   entity          = "works",   has_content.pdf = TRUE,   is_xpac         = TRUE,   from_publication_date = "2024-01-01",   options = list(include_xpac = TRUE) ) # After fetching and extracting IDs from the metadata: # result <- pro_download_content(ids = xpac_ids, format = "pdf")

Enable XPAC passing include_xpac = TRUE via options parameter: retrieve XPAC works (excluding standard works), combine include_xpac = TRUE is_xpac filter: XPAC works can combined filter search mode, including semantic search: XPAC work IDs can passed directly pro_download_content() — include_xpac discovery-phase parameter needed download time. find XPAC works also downloadable full-text, combine filters: See OpenAlex XPAC documentation details.

title: Working with OpenAlex Snapshots
text: OpenAlex provides complete data snapshots can downloaded processed locally. approach offers several advantages API queries: - rate limits: Process millions records without API restrictions - Reproducibility: Work fixed point--time dataset - Speed: Local queries significantly faster API calls (many cases, especially larger result corp[ora concerns)]) - Offline access: internet connection required download vignette guides : Setting environment Downloading OpenAlex snapshot Converting parquet format Building indexes fast lookups Querying local corpus

title: Working with OpenAlex Snapshots
text: complex analytical queries, use DuckDB directly:
code: library(openalexPro)  # Look up specific works by OpenAlex ID (returns data frame) works <- lookup_by_id(   index_file = "/Volumes/external-drive/openalex/parquet/works_id_idx.parquet",   ids = c("W2741809807", "W2100837269") )  # For millions of IDs, write directly to parquet instead of loading into memory lookup_by_id(   index_file = "/Volumes/external-drive/openalex/parquet/works_id_idx.parquet",   ids = large_id_vector,   output = "filtered_works",   workers = 3 ) library(duckdb) library(DBI)  con <- dbConnect(duckdb())  # Register parquet files as a view dbExecute(   con,   "   CREATE VIEW works AS   SELECT * FROM parquet_scan('/Volumes/external-drive/openalex/parquet/works/*.parquet') " )  # Run analytical queries result <- dbGetQuery(   con,   "   SELECT publication_year, COUNT(*) as n_works   FROM works   WHERE publication_year >= 2020   GROUP BY publication_year   ORDER BY publication_year " )  dbDisconnect(con)

title: Working with OpenAlex Snapshots
text: OpenAlex releases new snapshots regularly. Makefile supports update workflows via dedicated timestamp targets. shows current state S3 bucket including total size file count. shows current values configuration variables, including RELEASE_DATE read RELEASE_NOTES.txt. Note: snapshot_timestamp parquet_timestamp read release date RELEASE_NOTES.txt snapshot directory. Run snapshot_timestamp downloading new snapshot, parquet_timestamp re-converting, old release date still available.
code: make snapshot_info make help # 1. Archive the current snapshot with its release date #    (renames openalex-snapshot → openalex-snapshot-YYYY-MM-DD) make snapshot_timestamp  # 2. Download the new snapshot make snapshot  # 3. Archive the current parquet directory with the old release date make parquet_timestamp  # 4. Re-convert to parquet (deletes parquet dir first, then converts) make parquet SAMPLE_SIZE=10000  # 5. Rebuild indexes make parquet_index

title: Snapshot Conversion: Schema Inference and Parquet Writing
text: snapshot_to_parquet() converts OpenAlex NDJSON snapshot (hundreds gzip-compressed files) Parquet dataset. vignette explains internal mechanics detail, focusing two-stage pipeline: Schema inference — derive single, consistent column specification sample source files. Per-file conversion — write .gz file .parquet file using specification. Understanding stages helps tune parameters, interpret progress messages, manage schema cache, use infer_json_schema() directly workflows.

title: Snapshot Conversion: Schema Inference and Parquet Writing
text: OpenAlex snapshot stores data NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON), one record per line. JSON schemaless: field appears number one file may absent (thus typed differently auto-inference) another. DuckDB’s read_json_auto() infers column types independently per file. inferred types differ across files, reading collection parquet files together arrow::open_dataset() DuckDB’s parquet_scan() causes type-mismatch errors : solution infer , apply everywhere: run type inference sample files, compute single widened type column, write every parquet file fixed schema. files dataset share identical schema can read together without errors.

Columns appear first-seen order collected schemas (alphabetically). preserves natural field order OpenAlex schema maintains backward compatibility unified schema reused across runs. works dataset two specific workarounds applied schema inference: Individual work records (particularly long author lists) can exceed DuckDB’s default JSON object size limit. maximum_object_size option extended schema inference conversion: OpenAlex encodes abstracts inverted index: JSON object mapping word list positions, e.g. {"": [0], "quick": [1], "": [3], "": [7]}. JSON keys case-sensitive, "" "" distinct. DuckDB, however, folds struct field names lowercase auto-inferring STRUCT types, causing collision: schema inference, snapshot_to_parquet() overrides inferred type column VARCHAR: type VARCHAR, DuckDB reads entire JSON value raw text string without parsing keys struct fields, collision occurs. data fully preserved. access individual entries, parse jsonlite:
code: columns_clause <- infer_json_schema(   con             = con,            # shared DuckDB connection   files           = gz_files,       # all .gz files for this dataset   sample_size     = sample_size,    # how many files to sample (default 20)   extra_options   = ndjson_options, # dataset-specific DuckDB options   verbose         = TRUE,   schema_cache_dir = file.path(parquet_ds, ".schema_cache") ) {'id': 'VARCHAR', 'display_name': 'VARCHAR', 'works_count': 'BIGINT', ...} COPY (SELECT * FROM read_json('file.gz', columns = {'id': 'VARCHAR', ...})) TO 'file.parquet' (FORMAT PARQUET, COMPRESSION SNAPPY, ROW_GROUP_SIZE 100000) if (!is.null(sample_size) && sample_size > 0 && length(files) > sample_size) {   files <- sample(files, sample_size) } DESCRIBE SELECT * FROM   read_json_auto(['file.gz'], union_by_name = true, ignore_errors = true) # All files: 'id' = VARCHAR → unified: VARCHAR # Some files: 'apc_paid' = VARCHAR (nulls only) # Other files: 'apc_paid' = STRUCT(value BIGINT, currency VARCHAR, ...) # → unified: STRUCT(value BIGINT, currency VARCHAR, ...) # File A: 'location' = STRUCT(country VARCHAR) # File B: 'location' = STRUCT(country VARCHAR, city VARCHAR, lat DOUBLE) # → unified: STRUCT(country VARCHAR, city VARCHAR, lat DOUBLE) TINYINT < SMALLINT < INTEGER (= INT) < BIGINT < HUGEINT < FLOAT < DOUBLE # Some files: 'cited_by_count' = INTEGER # Other files: 'cited_by_count' = BIGINT # → unified: BIGINT # Some files: 'updated_date' = DATE # Other files: 'updated_date' = VARCHAR # → unified: VARCHAR ndjson_options <- if (data_set == "works") {   ", maximum_object_size=1000000000"   # 1 GB } else {   "" } Error: duplicate key "as" columns_clause <- gsub(   "'abstract_inverted_index':\\s*'[^']*'",   "'abstract_inverted_index': 'VARCHAR'",   columns_clause ) library(arrow) library(jsonlite)  works <- open_dataset("parquet/works") row   <- works |> filter(id == "W2741809807") |> collect()  aii   <- fromJSON(row$abstract_inverted_index[1]) # aii is now a named list: list("The" = 0, "quick" = 1, "as" = 3, "As" = 7)

Delete unified_schema.csv re-infer suspect schema changed. Makefile parquet target runs clean_parquet first, deletes entire parquet directory including .schema_cache/. Every make parquet run therefore always re-infers schema scratch. cache useful calling snapshot_to_parquet() directly R resume interrupted conversion, intentionally reuse schema previous run.

Loaded cached unified schema with 47 columns (delete .../unified_schema.csv to re-infer) rm parquet/works/.schema_cache/unified_schema.csv # After a successful conversion, copy the schema to a safe location cp parquet/works/.schema_cache/unified_schema.csv ~/schemas/works_schema.csv  # Start a new conversion (e.g. after downloading an updated snapshot) # but first restore the cached schema to skip inference: mkdir -p parquet/works/.schema_cache cp ~/schemas/works_schema.csv parquet/works/.schema_cache/unified_schema.csv  # Now run conversion — schema inference will be skipped snapshot_to_parquet(   snapshot_dir  = "/Volumes/openalex/openalex-snapshot",   parquet_dir   = "/Volumes/openalex/parquet",   data_sets     = "works",   workers       = 4,   memory_limit  = "8GB" ) schema <- read.csv("parquet/works/.schema_cache/unified_schema.csv")  # Inspect the schema head(schema) #         col_name              col_type # 1             id               VARCHAR # 2   display_name               VARCHAR # 3    works_count                BIGINT # ...  # Override a type if needed, e.g. force a column to VARCHAR schema$col_type[schema$col_name == "some_column"] <- "VARCHAR"  # Write back — this will be loaded on the next run write.csv(schema, "parquet/works/.schema_cache/unified_schema.csv",           row.names = FALSE)

title: Snapshot Conversion: Schema Inference and Parquet Writing
text: columns_clause ready, snapshot_to_parquet() converts .gz file individually using convert_json_to_parquet(): Key properties: One DuckDB connection per worker: future worker opens connection, locking conflicts. Snappy compression: good balance speed size. Row group size 100 000: controls granularity predicate pushdown memory use reads. Directory structure preserved: output paths mirror input hive partition structure (updated_date=2024-01-15/part_000.parquet), dataset immediately usable Arrow’s partition-aware reader. Resume support: already-present .parquet files skipped conversion starts, interrupted run can restarted. Set workers use multiple future::multisession workers: worker gets DuckDB connection memory_limit temp_directory settings. Schema inference always runs sequentially main process (uses single shared connection).
code: COPY (SELECT * FROM read_json('file.gz', columns = {'id': 'VARCHAR', ...})) TO 'file.parquet' (FORMAT PARQUET, COMPRESSION SNAPPY, ROW_GROUP_SIZE 100000) snapshot_to_parquet(   snapshot_dir  = "/Volumes/openalex/openalex-snapshot",   parquet_dir   = "/Volumes/openalex/parquet",   data_sets     = "works",   workers       = 4,   memory_limit  = "8GB",   temp_directory = "/tmp" )

text: infer_json_schema() exported can used standalone set NDJSON files, just OpenAlex snapshots: schema_cache_dir argument accepts writable directory. Per-file CSVs named <parent_dir>_<filename_without_gz>.csv; parent directory follow updated_date= convention raw directory name used.
code: library(DBI) library(duckdb) library(openalexPro)  # Open a DuckDB connection and load the JSON extension con <- dbConnect(duckdb()) dbExecute(con, "LOAD json")  # Collect files files <- list.files(   "path/to/ndjson",   pattern    = "\\.gz$",   recursive  = TRUE,   full.names = TRUE )  # Infer schema from up to 50 files, cache in a dedicated directory columns_clause <- infer_json_schema(   con             = con,   files           = files,   sample_size     = 50,   verbose         = TRUE,   schema_cache_dir = "path/to/cache" )  cat(columns_clause) # {'id': 'VARCHAR', 'name': 'VARCHAR', 'count': 'BIGINT', ...}  # Use it in your own query sql <- sprintf(   "COPY (SELECT * FROM read_json('data.gz', columns = %s)) TO 'data.parquet'",   columns_clause ) dbExecute(con, sql)  dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)

title: Infer unified JSON schema using DuckDB — infer_json_schema
text: `schema_cache_dir` provided, two levels caching apply: - **Unified schema** (`unified_schema.csv`): present, loaded returned   immediately — DuckDB queries needed. Delete file force   re-inference. - **Per-file schemas** (`<update_date>_<part_name>.csv`): file's schema   saved inferred. restart, already-cached files skipped,   enabling mid-run resume large file sets.

title: Infer unified JSON schema using DuckDB — infer_json_schema
text: column different types across files, unified type chosen rules (order): 1. identical → keep -. 2. `STRUCT`/`LIST`/`MAP` vs simpler type → complex type wins. 3. Multiple `STRUCT` types → pick one fields. 4. Numeric conflicts → widest type wins    (`TINYINT < SMALLINT < INTEGER < BIGINT < HUGEINT < FLOAT < DOUBLE`). 5. Fallback → `VARCHAR`.

title: Build an OpenAlex request (httr2) — pro_query
text: entity Character; one "works", "authors", "venues", "institutions", "concepts", "publishers", "funders". id Optional ID vector IDs (e.g., "W1775749144"). single ID provided, fetches one entity directly. multiple IDs provided, automatically moved ids.openalex filter. search Optional full-text search string. Applies stemming stop-word removal. Supports boolean operators (, , uppercase), quoted phrases ("exact phrase"), proximity ("word1 word2"~N), wildcards (*, ?), fuzzy matching (term~N). Replaces deprecated filter = field.search:keyword syntax. search.exact Optional full-text search without stemming stop-word removal. Supports boolean/phrase/wildcard syntax search. Use need match exact word forms (e.g. "surgery" match "surgical"). search.semantic Optional semantic (AI-powered) search string. Uses embeddings match conceptual meaning rather exact keywords. Limited 1 request per second returns 50 results per query. group_by Optional field group (facets), e.g. "type". select Optional character vector fields return. options Optional named list additional query parameters (e.g., list(per_page = 200, sort = "cited_by_count:desc", cursor = "*", sample = 100)). endpoint Base API URL. Defaults "https://api.openalex.org". chunk_limit Number DOIS ids per chunk chunked. Default: 50 ... Filters named arguments. Values may scalars vectors (vectors collapsed "|" express ).

title: Build an OpenAlex request (httr2) — pro_query
text: three search parameters (search, search.exact, search.semantic) accept query string. search search.exact, following syntax supported: Boolean: biodiversity finance, climate weather,     ocean pollution (operators must uppercase). Exact phrase: "biodiversity finance" (double quotes). Proximity: "biodiversity finance"~5 (words within 5 positions). Wildcard: bio* (zero characters), organi?ation. Fuzzy: biodiversty~1 (allows 1 character edit). search.semantic use keyword syntax; pass natural-language phrase even full abstract. returns 50 results per call.

title: openalexPro 0.6.0
text: Added pro_rate_limit_status() query OpenAlex rate-limit endpoint (GET /rate-limit). Returns full rate-limit JSON invisibly (daily budget, used, remaining, prepaid balance, per-endpoint costs, reset time). Prints human-readable summary via message() verbose = TRUE (default). Returns FALSE missing invalid API key, NULL network error, callers can distinguish auth problems transient failures. pro_validate_credentials() refactored use pro_rate_limit_status() internally instead making separate pro_count() request. Behaviour return value unchanged. Added pro_download_content() download full-text PDFs (format = "pdf") TEI XML (format = "grobid-xml") OpenAlex content endpoint (content.openalex.org). Accepts vector work IDs, supports parallel downloads via workers, returns data frame per-file status ("ok" / "not_found" / "error"). Note: content downloads cost $0.01 per file. Added search.exact search.semantic parameters pro_query(), matching new OpenAlex search API: search.exact: searches without stemming stop-word removal; supports boolean operators, quoted phrases, proximity (~N), wildcards. search.semantic: AI embedding-based search matches conceptual meaning rather keywords (max 50 results, max 1 req/sec). search: now documented support full boolean/phrase/wildcard syntax addition existing stemmed matching. Exported infer_json_schema() direct use. Infers unified DuckDB columns clause set JSON/NDJSON files via per-file DESCRIBE queries type-widening optional two-level disk caching (schema_cache_dir).

title: openalexPro 0.6.0
text: Fixed Windows path-normalization failures snapshot_to_parquet(), build_corpus_index(), lookup_by_id(), pro_request_jsonl_parquet(). Windows, normalizePath() can return 8.3 short names (e.g. RUNNER~1) tempdir()-derived paths list.files() DuckDB resolve long names (runneradmin). Resume detection snapshot_to_parquet() used %% paths mixed separators (\ vs /), causing already-converted files reconverted. build_corpus_index() embedded snapshot_dir (\) inside DuckDB regexp_replace pattern, never matched — full absolute path stored index later doubled lookup_by_id(). pro_request_jsonl_parquet() used normalizePath string comparison detect subdirectories, always failed, placing every output file spurious query=<dirname> subdirectory. Fixes: (1) normalize separators / gsub("\\\\", "/", ...) sides %% comparisons; (2) compute relative paths R using path-depth counting (strsplit(path, "/") indexed extraction) rather string-matching absolute paths — immune 8.3 vs long-name differences; pass relative path SQL literal build_corpus_index() instead computing inside DuckDB regex.

title: openalexPro 0.5.0
text: Fixed vignette parse errors pro_query.qmd (malformed code block closings). Fixed --memory crash snapshot_to_parquet() sample_size exceeded number available files (e.g. sample_size = 10000 1981 works files). Schema inference now processes one file time instead single bulk DuckDB query. Fixed duplicate key "" crash converting works dataset. abstract_inverted_index now stored VARCHAR (raw JSON string) rather STRUCT. DuckDB folds struct field names lowercase, causing collision valid JSON keys "" "" field. Storing VARCHAR avoids struct parsing entirely preserves data.
